skill.name: vitest
skill.description: Vitest guidance for writing, configuring, mocking, filtering, and maintaining tests in Vite-based JavaScript and TypeScript projects.
skill.summary: Use Vitest documentation guidance to create faster, clearer tests for Vite, TypeScript, and browser-like environments.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, script, network, and environment patterns, but review found they are Vitest documentation examples and metadata. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious command execution, or hidden runtime behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Examples Are Documentation
low_findings.description: Static command findings point to Vitest CLI and package installation examples. They are fenced markdown snippets and generation metadata, not commands executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is markdown documentation and fenced examples for Vitest, not executable skill code. The surrounding headings explicitly describe CLI usage and environment package setup.

low_findings.title: Script Patterns Are Test Examples
low_findings.description: Dynamic import, DOM assignment, VM import, and browser storage references appear in Vitest environment or mocking examples. No user input sink or active execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside explanatory markdown sections for jsdom, VM environments, and vi.doMock. No skill file invokes these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: Network And Secret Terms Are Mock Data
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs and API key strings are source links, localhost examples, or mocked test values. The review found no request that sends secrets to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documentation references or fixture URLs, and API_KEY is set to a test value with vi.stubEnv. No exfiltration code was found.

low_findings.title: Blocker Keywords Are Benign Markdown Matches
low_findings.description: C2 and weak-crypto detections appear to match ordinary metadata, headings, Git SHA text, and Vitest API words. No cryptographic implementation or command-and-control behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal documentation context at the cited lines. The repository contains markdown reference material, not executable malware logic.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time translating Vitest documentation into correct test patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for Vitest APIs, configuration, mocking, coverage, and fixtures.
actual_capabilities: Explains Vitest configuration with vite.config.ts and vitest.config.ts.
Guides use of test, describe, expect, hooks, and concurrent test APIs.
Provides patterns for vi mocks, spies, fake timers, and environment stubs.
Covers coverage settings, snapshots, filtering, tags, and watch mode commands.
Supports browser-like test environments such as jsdom and happy-dom.
Includes TypeScript type testing with expectTypeOf and assertType.
limitations: It is based on Vitest 3.x documentation generated on 2026-01-28.
It does not run tests or inspect project failures by itself.
It does not replace project-specific test design decisions.
It may need verification against newer Vitest releases.

use_cases.title: Add Unit Tests To A Vite App
use_cases.description: Create focused tests using test, describe, expect, setup files, and project configuration.

use_cases.title: Convert Jest Patterns To Vitest
use_cases.description: Map common Jest-style assertions, mocks, snapshots, and lifecycle hooks to Vitest APIs.

use_cases.title: Improve CI Test Reliability
use_cases.description: Use run mode, filtering, retries, coverage, sharding, and concurrency guidance for stable automation.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Vitest skill to write unit tests for this function. Include describe, test, and expect patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Vitest For Vite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Vitest skill to configure Vitest for a Vite TypeScript project with jsdom and setup files.

prompt_templates.title: Mock Modules And Timers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Vitest skill to mock this module, add fake timer tests, and explain cleanup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design A CI Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Vitest skill to propose CI settings for coverage, sharding, retries, filtering, and snapshot handling.

output_examples.input: A user asks for tests around a TypeScript utility function.
output_examples.output: A concise test plan with grouped cases, expected assertions, edge cases, and Vitest API choices.

output_examples.input: A user needs Vitest configuration for browser component tests.
output_examples.output: A configuration outline covering jsdom, setup files, globals, coverage, and package requirements.

output_examples.input: A user asks why mocked modules are leaking between tests.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis of mock hoisting, cleanup methods, and where to restore spies or environment stubs.

best_practices: Use context expect in concurrent tests and snapshot assertions.
Restore mocks, timers, globals, and environment stubs after each test.
Run Vitest in single-run mode for CI and lint-staged workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not leave test.only or describe.only in committed test files.
Do not mix real timers with fake timer assertions without cleanup.
Do not rely on browser APIs without selecting a matching test environment.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code answer Vitest testing, mocking, configuration, coverage, and filtering questions.

faq.question: Does it execute Vitest commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent must choose whether to run commands.

faq.question: Can it help migrate from Jest?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Jest-compatible assertions, mocks, spies, snapshots, hooks, and common Vitest differences.

faq.question: Does it cover browser tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains jsdom, happy-dom, environment options, per-file environments, and browser-like API testing.

faq.question: Does it include TypeScript testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers TypeScript support and type-level tests with expectTypeOf and assertType.

faq.question: What version is it based on?
faq.answer: The skill states it is based on Vitest 3.x documentation generated on 2026-01-28.
